qa_pairs.question: What are the characteristics of a general-purpose compute cluster, and what types of nodes does it typically include?
qa_pairs.answer: A general-purpose compute cluster is designed to support a wide variety of job types and is composed of a mixture of different nodes. These nodes are broadly classified as base nodes (typically about 4GB of memory per core), large-memory nodes (typically more than 8GB memory per core), and GPU nodes (which contain graphic processing units). All clusters also have large, high-performance storage attached.

qa_pairs.question: Which specific compute clusters are currently in production, according to the provided list?
qa_pairs.answer: The compute clusters currently in production are Fir (General-purpose), Narval (General-purpose), Nibi (General-purpose), Rorqual (General-purpose), and Trillium (Large parallel).

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the Pan-Canadian AI Compute Environment (PAICE) clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Pan-Canadian AI Compute Environment (PAICE) clusters are systems dedicated to meeting the current and emerging artificial intelligence (AI) needs of Canada’s research community.
